annotations.short-term: Martin Atkinson blows his whistle and it's Dele Alli (Tottenham) who is penalised for an offensive foul. Good decision by the referee. He had shown great energy in attack but was too aggressive in his challenge.

history.short-term: Harry Kane (Tottenham) finds himself beyond the last defender, but the game is stopped by the referee after the linesman signals for offside. He had timed his run well but was just a step too early, as the assistant referee raised his flag immediately.
history.long-term: They have been displaying aggressive movements in their attacking plays but have not been able to capitalize on scoring opportunities due to overly aggressive challenges and offside decisions.

history.short-term: Martin Atkinson has a clear sight and sees a foul from Mathieu Debuchy (Arsenal). The full-back was slightly late with his challenge, giving them a free-kick in a decent attacking position.
history.long-term: The referee has been actively enforcing discipline, calling out fouls on both sides to maintain a fair game. The players need to balance their aggression with caution.
